title: SaaS Industry Statistics 2026
description: Essential statistics on the SaaS industry including market size, growth metrics, pricing trends, churn benchmarks, and public company performance.

stats.label: Net Revenue Retention for Top-Quartile SaaS
Free Tier to Paid Conversion Rate

sections.title: Market Size and Growth
sections.body: The SaaS market continues its robust growth trajectory, with 18.4% year-over-year expansion driven by cloud migration, AI integration, and new category creation. Nearly two-thirds of enterprise software is now delivered via SaaS, with the remaining on-premises and hybrid deployments declining 8% annually. North America remains the largest market, though Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region at 26% CAGR.

sections.title: Pricing and Revenue Metrics
sections.body: Usage-based pricing has become the dominant model for new SaaS products, while established vendors increasingly adopt hybrid pricing combining per-seat, consumption, and AI surcharge components. Average per-seat costs rose 14% year-over-year, but contract discounts have tightened as vendors prioritize predictable recurring revenue. AI feature surcharges have become standard, adding average costs of $25 per user per month.

sections.title: Retention and Churn Benchmarks
sections.body: Net revenue retention (NRR) has become the most-watched SaaS metric, with top-quartile public companies maintaining 92% NRR through expansion revenue. Enterprise churn remains significantly lower than SMB churn, reflecting deeper integration and switching costs. AI software retention has notably improved from 76% to 84% as tools become more embedded in daily workflows.

sections.title: Public SaaS Company Performance
sections.body: Public SaaS company valuations have stabilized at a median 7.8x enterprise value to revenue multiple after the correction from 2021 highs. Profitability has improved dramatically, with median EBITDA margins reaching 24%. The trend toward capital efficiency is driving later IPOs, with companies reaching a median $12.4 million ARR before going public. Self-serve revenue models continue to expand, with 38% of public SaaS companies generating over half their revenue through self-serve channels.
